title: Oxytropis muricata
uses: Oxytropis muricata has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. In some regions, the plant is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The roots and leaves of Oxytropis muricata are sometimes used in traditional remedies for treating digestive issues and fever. However, more research is required to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes. Further studies are needed to fully understand the potential benefits and risks associated with using Oxytropis muricata.
taxonomy: Oxytropis muricata, a species of flowering plant, belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the tribe Fabaceae subtribe Genistidae. The genus Oxytropis is further divided into several sections and subsections based on its morphological and geographical characteristics. Oxytropis muricata specifically is classified under the subgenus Oxytropis. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the Fabaceae family.
benefits: Oxytropis muricata, also known as spiny knapweed, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The plant's roots and leaves are often used to make medicinal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are thought to have a range of therapeutic effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits and potential uses of this plant.

side_effects: Oxytropis muricata is known to be toxic due to the presence of glycosides, which can cause a range of side effects. Ingestion of the plant can lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The glycosides in Oxytropis muricata can also cause cardiovascular problems, including lowered blood pressure and heart rate. Furthermore, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can cause liver and kidney damage. It is essential to handle the plant with caution and avoid consumption to prevent potential harm.
